since_last: 39% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +2.78%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 39% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +0.04% · BTC -2.33% · Gold +1.43% · Nasdaq100 +0.90%. 39% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +2.78%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +2.78%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -3.05%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +17.96%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +17.96%, AMAT +11.62%, TER +9.02%, KLAC +8.17%, QCOM +7.70%. Laggards: INOD -11.09%, DELL -5.77%, AAPL -5.54%, PLTR -5.37%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -1.61% at $152.05
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -1.61% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
